nodes.name: app.ts
nodes.summary: Creates the Express application for market-rest, wiring CORS, JSON parsing, health checks, Swagger UI, API routes, and a final 404 handler.
nodes.languageNotes: Express application composition keeps the server bootstrap separate from route and process lifecycle startup.

nodes.name: database.ts
nodes.summary: Configures the shared MySQL connection pool from environment settings and exposes a startup ping used by the REST server bootstrap.

nodes.name: env.ts
nodes.summary: Loads dotenv configuration and centralizes required runtime settings for server, database, Kafka, Redis, and backend command URIs.
nodes.languageNotes: The exported config object gives TypeScript callers a typed application configuration surface via typeof config.

nodes.name: kafka.ts
nodes.summary: Builds a singleton KafkaJS client and exposes factory helpers for producers and consumers using configured brokers and group IDs.

nodes.name: redis.ts
nodes.summary: Creates the shared Redis client from configured host, port, password, and database values, including connection and error logging hooks.

nodes.name: KafkaConsumer.ts
nodes.summary: Wraps a KafkaJS consumer with connection readiness, topic subscription, message handling, and disconnect operations for the market-rest service.

nodes.name: ahpConfigController.ts
nodes.summary: Defines Express handlers for reading AHP configuration directly and creating or updating AHP settings through the queue service.

nodes.name: portfolioController.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the portfolio allocation endpoint handler, validates the required allocation request fields, and delegates allocation work to the queue service.

nodes.name: stockController.ts
nodes.summary: Defines stock REST handlers for lookup, ID listing, CRUD-style queue commands, industry ratio updates, and valuation recalculation.

nodes.name: stockYearDataController.ts
nodes.summary: Defines stock-year data REST handlers with year parsing, direct reads, and queued create, update, and delete commands.

nodes.name: subscriptionController.ts
nodes.summary: Defines subscription REST handlers for creating subscriptions through Kafka-backed queueing and listing available subscription plans.

nodes.name: userController.ts
nodes.summary: Defines user REST handlers for profile lookup, account mutation, authentication, and favorite-stock management with cache invalidation after changes.

nodes.name: swagger.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the OpenAPI 3.0 Swagger specification for market-rest endpoints, request schemas, response descriptions, and API tags.
nodes.languageNotes: The specification is embedded as a TypeScript object consumed by swagger-jsdoc and served from the Express app.

nodes.name: AhpConfigDto.ts
nodes.summary: Declares the AHP configuration transfer shape with user ownership plus serialized criteria, pairwise matrix, and weight data.

nodes.name: PortfolioAllocationRequest.ts
nodes.summary: Declares the portfolio allocation request payload with user, budget, stock-count, and optional lot-size fields.

nodes.name: ResponseDto.ts
nodes.summary: Declares the generic service response envelope with success state, error metadata, and optional typed data.

nodes.name: StockDto.ts
nodes.summary: Declares the stock transfer shape, combining stock identity, market ratios, current pricing, and nested yearly financial data.

nodes.name: StockYearDataDto.ts
nodes.summary: Declares yearly stock financial metrics used for valuation, including income, cash flow, equity, discount rates, and ratio outputs.

nodes.name: SubscriptionDto.ts
nodes.summary: Declares subscription status values and the subscription transfer shape used by subscription queue and controller flows.

nodes.name: UserDetailDto.ts
nodes.summary: Declares the enriched user detail transfer shape with account fields, subscriptions, and optional AHP configuration.

nodes.name: UserDto.ts
nodes.summary: Declares the user command payload shape for account creation, updates, login, admin state, and favorite-stock actions.

nodes.name: message.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the generic Kafka message envelope class used to populate payload metadata and a UTC+7 timestamp when publishing commands.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Bootstraps the market-rest process by creating the app, checking MySQL, connecting Redis, starting the HTTP listener, and handling shutdown signals.

nodes.name: KafkaEnvelope.ts
nodes.summary: Declares the generic Kafka envelope contract shared by message DTOs, including source, event, URI, timestamp, and payload fields.

nodes.name: AhpConfigEntity.ts
nodes.summary: Declares the persisted AHP configuration entity shape with a user relation and serialized AHP preference data.

nodes.name: createApp
nodes.summary: Builds and returns the configured Express application with middleware, health checks, Swagger UI, API routing, and 404 handling.

nodes.name: initDatabase
nodes.summary: Acquires a MySQL connection, pings it to verify startup connectivity, logs success, and releases the connection.

nodes.name: getKafka
nodes.summary: Returns the shared KafkaJS client, lazily constructing it from the current Kafka configuration on first use.

nodes.name: createProducer
nodes.summary: Creates a KafkaJS producer from the shared Kafka client for queue-backed command publishing.

nodes.name: createConsumer
nodes.summary: Creates a KafkaJS consumer from the shared Kafka client using the configured consumer group ID.

nodes.name: KafkaConsumer
nodes.summary: Encapsulates KafkaJS consumer setup, readiness waiting, handler execution, and disconnect lifecycle for the configured market-rest topic.

nodes.name: getAhpConfigByUserId
nodes.summary: Validates the userId path parameter, reads a user AHP configuration, and returns 400, 404, or the config payload.

nodes.name: createAhpConfig
nodes.summary: Converts the request body to an AHP config DTO and queues a create command with success-based HTTP status mapping.

nodes.name: updateAhpConfig
nodes.summary: Converts the request body to an AHP config DTO and queues an update command with success-based HTTP status mapping.

nodes.name: allocatePortfolio
nodes.summary: Validates required allocation request fields and queues the portfolio allocation command for asynchronous processing.

nodes.name: getStockById
nodes.summary: Validates the stockId path parameter, loads a stock by ID, and maps missing or failed lookups to HTTP error responses.

nodes.name: getAllStocksId
nodes.summary: Reads and returns the available stock IDs through the stock service.

nodes.name: createStock
nodes.summary: Queues stock creation from the request DTO and maps the queue response to created or bad-request status codes.

nodes.name: updateStock
nodes.summary: Queues stock updates from the request DTO and maps the queue response to success or bad-request status codes.

nodes.name: deleteStock
nodes.summary: Queues stock deletion from the request DTO and maps the queue response to success or bad-request status codes.

nodes.name: updateIndustryRatios
nodes.summary: Queues updates to stock industry valuation ratios and returns the queue result to the client.

nodes.name: forceRecalculateValuations
nodes.summary: Queues a full valuation recalculation command and returns the result status to the client.

nodes.name: getStockYearData
nodes.summary: Validates stockId and numeric year parameters, loads stock-year financial data, and returns missing data as 404.

nodes.name: createStockYearData
nodes.summary: Parses the year path parameter and queues creation of stock-year financial data for that year.

nodes.name: updateStockYearData
nodes.summary: Parses the year path parameter and queues updates to stock-year financial data for that year.

nodes.name: deleteStockYearData
nodes.summary: Queues deletion of stock-year financial data from the request DTO and returns the queue result.

nodes.name: createSubscription
nodes.summary: Queues subscription creation from the request DTO and returns created or bad-request status based on command success.

nodes.name: getSubscriptionPlans
nodes.summary: Reads all subscription plans and returns them in a response data envelope.

nodes.name: getFavoriteStocks
nodes.summary: Validates a userId path parameter and returns the user favorite stock IDs from the user service.

nodes.name: getUserDetail
nodes.summary: Validates a userId path parameter, loads enriched user detail, and returns 404 when the user is absent.

nodes.name: createUser
nodes.summary: Queues user creation from the request DTO and maps the queue result to created or bad-request responses.

nodes.name: updateUser
nodes.summary: Queues user updates from the request DTO and maps the queue result to success or bad-request responses.

nodes.name: deleteUser
nodes.summary: Queues user deletion and invalidates favorite-stock cache entries after a successful delete for a known user ID.

nodes.name: updatePassword
nodes.summary: Queues a password update command for a user payload and returns the queue result status.

nodes.name: loginUser
nodes.summary: Queues a login command and maps unsuccessful authentication to HTTP 401.

nodes.name: addFavoriteStock
nodes.summary: Queues a favorite-stock addition and invalidates the cached favorite list after success.

nodes.name: removeFavoriteStock
nodes.summary: Queues a favorite-stock removal and invalidates the cached favorite list after success.

nodes.name: Message
nodes.summary: Represents a typed Kafka envelope and fills in a UTC+7 ISO timestamp when one is not supplied by the caller.

nodes.name: startServer
nodes.summary: Initializes MySQL and Redis, starts the HTTP listener on the configured port, and exits the process on startup failure.
